name: Fastmail MCP Server
what_it_does: Fastmail MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Fastmail email accounts via the JMAP protocol — reading, sending, searching, and organizing emails, managing folders and contacts, and integrating Fastmail's privacy-focused email service into agent-driven communication workflows.
use_cases: Reading and summarizing emails from communication agents
Sending emails and replies from automated workflow agents
Searching email history from information retrieval agents
Organizing emails into folders from inbox management agents
Managing contacts in Fastmail from CRM agents
Processing newsletters and notifications from filtering agents
not_for: Non-Fastmail email providers (use Gmail MCP, Proton MCP, etc.)
High-volume marketing email sending (Fastmail ToS restrictions)
best_when: A Fastmail user wants AI assistance with email management — using Fastmail's modern JMAP API for more reliable email operations than legacy IMAP.
avoid_when: You use Gmail, Outlook, or another email provider — use the appropriate MCP.

auth.notes: Fastmail API token required. Generate from Fastmail Settings → API tokens. Scoped tokens available (Mail, Contacts, Calendar). Token provides JMAP API access.

pricing.notes: Fastmail subscription required. Relatively affordable email service. JMAP API included with subscription. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: Fastmail API: generous limits for personal use

known_agent_gotchas: JMAP protocol knowledge is less common than IMAP — community support smaller
Fastmail subscription required — minor cost barrier
Community MCP from individual contributor — limited documentation
Scoped API tokens recommended — don't use full-access tokens
Email content is highly sensitive — implement appropriate privacy controls
JMAP pagination differs from IMAP — cursor-based
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Scoped API tokens. Community MCP. Email data is sensitive. Fastmail is privacy-focused service.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. Scoped tokens. Privacy-focused email. Community MCP. Sensitive email data.
